title: Oil Prices Surge as US-Iran Peace Talks Stall
summary: Oil prices increased by about 2% after plans for a second round of peace talks between the US and Iran stalled. US President Donald Trump cancelled sending a team to Pakistan for negotiations with Iranian officials. The global benchmark Brent crude rose to $109.33 per barrel, while US crude reached $96.78. The conflict has effectively closed the Strait of Hormuz, a crucial waterway through which about one-fifth of the world's crude oil and liquefied natural gas passes. Iranian Foreign Minister Seyed Abbas Araghchi has been engaging in discussions with Oman and is set to meet Russian President Vladimir Putin. Experts warn that if the strait remains closed for more than a few weeks, the impact on global supply chains could be severe, affecting products from bin bags to medicine. Oil traders are cautious and waiting for credible evidence of a lasting peace before reacting to headlines.

a narrow strait between Iran and Oman through which a significant portion of global oil passes
the network of production and transportation that delivers products to consumers
a standard against which prices are measured

background_read: The Strait of Hormuz is a narrow waterway between the Persian Gulf and the Gulf of Oman. It is a critical chokepoint for global oil shipments. About 20% of the world's oil passes through it. The US and Iran have been in conflict for years, and recent tensions have led to the closure of the strait. Peace talks are important to ensure the free flow of oil and prevent price spikes. Oil prices affect the cost of many goods, including gasoline, plastics, and medicines.
Article_Structure: The article reports on oil price increases due to stalled US-Iran peace talks. Main points: (1) Talks cancelled by Trump, (2) Strait of Hormuz closure affecting supply, (3) Expert warnings on supply chain impacts, (4) Trader skepticism. Purpose: To inform about the economic impact of geopolitical tensions. Evidence evaluation: The article uses specific price data and quotes from experts and officials, but lacks independent verification. Author credibility: BBC Business is a reputable source. Methodology: The article aggregates news from official statements, social media, and expert interviews.

perspectives.description: They are cautious and demand concrete evidence of peace before changing positions.
